narrative.headline: 2022 Nissan Sentra: steering leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 80 complaints with NHTSA. Steering is the most reported area at 39%, followed by the electrical system (30%) and forward collision avoidance (16%). No fires and no deaths are reported. Steering accounts for the only crash and the most injuries (3), so it is the area to scrutinize on a test drive.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: The top complaint by a wide margin: 31 reports (39%), tied to 1 crash and 3 injuries. Pay close attention to how the steering feels on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 24 reports (30%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. A common but lower-stakes complaint area.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 13 reports (16%), with 2 injuries and no crashes or fires. Worth checking that the driver-assist systems behave as expected.

issues.title: Wheels
issues.blurb: 8 reports (10%), linked to 1 crash and 3 injuries. A smaller complaint area but with serious outcomes attached.

issues.sample: Clicking / Popping noises when turning the steering wheel. Diagnosis bad insulator-engine mounting harness.   RPM / idle issues and jerking while driving. Diagnosis reprogrammed engine control module. Problem still present and worse, than prior to reprogram.   Cruise control slow
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The fewest complaints here: 7 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe noises and rough idle rather than safety incidents.
